findings.title: Dead ML Dependencies Causing Slow and Risky Deployments
findings.mechanism: Unused libraries (langchain, chromadb) are loaded into requirements.txt but disabled in the runtime environment. These cause 5-10 minutes of extra build overhead and memory issues on free hosting tiers.
findings.root_cause: Historical code remnants from older local Ollama/Chroma deployments are left in the requirements manifest.
findings.recommended_fix: Remove langchain and chromadb from requirements.txt.

findings.title: Silent Mock Leakage on AI Connection Failures
findings.mechanism: AI generators silently catch connection/API exceptions and return static mock templates without user warnings or administrative logging.
findings.root_cause: Overly permissive exception handling returning mock dictionaries/strings instead of failing with warnings.
findings.recommended_fix: Implement error alerts or warnings in the frontend to let the HOD know that the live AI response is unavailable and a mock fallback is displayed.

findings.title: Brittle JSON Answer Shaping without Markdown Stripping
findings.mechanism: System prompt relies on the LLM omitting markdown wrappers. If the model includes ```json ... ``` blocks, the JSON parser fails and triggers mock fallbacks.
findings.root_cause: Directly parsing raw LLM response text with json.loads() without sanitizing markdown code block fences.
findings.recommended_fix: Use a regex pattern to clean markdown formatting fences from the response string before attempting JSON deserialization.

ordered_fix_plan.why_now: Speeds up Render builds, decreases deployment pipeline failures, and reduces slug size.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Faster builds and zero chance of memory limits build crashes.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Sanitize JSON Parsing from LLM Responses
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: Ensures the application parses JSON correctly even if the model surrounds the block with code markdown blocks.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Expose AI Status / Warnings to Frontend
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: Improves transparency and lets administrators know if they are working with live generated content or hardcoded mocks.
